task_completion.description: A Firefox bookmark folder named 'Bibliography Sources' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Firefox bookmark toolbar, bookmark menu, or library window showing a folder explicitly named 'Bibliography Sources'.

task_completion.description: The DBLP pages for the 5 target papers are saved in the 'Bibliography Sources' bookmark folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open view of the 'Bibliography Sources' bookmark folder showing saved links to dblp.org for 'Attention is All You Need', 'Deep Residual Learning...', 'BERT...', 'Dropout...', and 'Adam...'.

task_completion.description: A file named deep_learning.bib is created in the ~/Documents/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor, file manager, or terminal showing the file path '/home/ga/Documents/deep_learning.bib' (or similar Documents path) being edited or saved.

task_completion.description: The .bib file contains the correct peer-reviewed BibTeX entries for 'Attention is All You Need' and 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of deep_learning.bib. Verify there is a BibTeX entry for Vaswani et al. showing publication at NeurIPS/NIPS (2017) and an entry for He et al. showing publication at CVPR (2016).

task_completion.description: The .bib file contains the correct peer-reviewed BibTeX entries for 'BERT', 'Dropout', and 'Adam'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of deep_learning.bib. Verify there are BibTeX entries for Devlin et al. at NAACL (2019), Srivastava et al. in JMLR (2014), and Kingma & Ba at ICLR (2015).

task_completion.description: None of the BibTeX entries are from ArXiv or CoRR.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine all visible BibTeX entries in the deep_learning.bib file. Ensure that none of the 'journal', 'booktitle', or 'url' fields contain 'CoRR' or 'arxiv'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Firefox GUI to navigate DBLP.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show Firefox open and navigating to dblp.org, rather than using terminal commands like curl or python scripts to fetch the data.

integrity.description: Bookmarks were created using the browser's graphical interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the bookmark star icon being clicked, the 'Add Bookmark' dialog box, or the Bookmark Manager window being used, rather than terminal manipulation of SQLite databases.

integrity.description: The BibTeX entries appear to be genuinely sourced from DBLP.
integrity.visual_evidence: DBLP BibTeX entries typically have distinct citation keys starting with 'DBLP:' (e.g., DBLP:conf/nips/VaswaniSPUJGKP17). The entries should match this format rather than looking manually typed or hallucinated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target papers and their correct venues: (1) 'Attention is All You Need' -> NeurIPS 2017; (2) 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition' -> CVPR 2016; (3) 'BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding' -> NAACL 2019; (4) 'Dropout: A Simple Way to Prevent Neural Networks from Overfitting' -> JMLR 2014; (5) 'Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization' -> ICLR 2015. The output file must be exactly at /home/ga/Documents/deep_learning.bib and the bookmark folder must be named exactly 'Bibliography Sources'.
